reasoning: Output B is the stronger forge report on nearly every dimension. Both outputs are well-structured and reach a FORGED verdict, but B demonstrates materially superior depth and rigor throughout. On contract probing, B probes every PC with a distinct adversarial angle and provides concrete RED test code and GREEN implementation snippets for each recycled bug — giving the reader verifiable proof that each fix is correct. Output A probes all 31 PCs but collapses PC-14 through PC-18 into a single summary block with no probe detail, and provides no test code. On bug discovery, B finds 9 bugs (vs A's 7), and crucially identifies two high-severity issues that A misses entirely: a SQL injection vector in the ORDER BY clause (ADVERSARY-1/PC-API-17) and an N+1 INSERT performance problem for line items (SCALE-1/PC-SVC-11). These are blocking-quality bugs that A classifies as non-blocking or misses. B also catches a subtle NaN bypass in its iteration 2 probing (PC-API-18) — demonstrating active re-probing of its own new PCs. The failure tracker in A is strong (a proper table across all 4 iterations), but B's iteration tracker table and per-check circuit breaker table achieve the same outcome more concisely. B's recycle log includes full RED test code and GREEN implementation code for all 9 bugs — the gold standard for forge evidence. A's recycle log summarises fixes without code. On file:line references, both outputs cite specific files and line numbers, but B consistently pairs them with actual code snippets. On non-blocking separation, both outputs clearly separate blocking recycled bugs from non-blocking improvements. Overall, B is more thorough, finds more bugs of higher severity, and provides stronger verifiable evidence of each fix.

strengths: All 7 mechanical checks run across all 4 iterations with per-check PASS/FAIL/FLAG status
Full per-PC contract probing of all 31 PCs with probe angle and result documented
All 5 adversarial lenses executed with named findings
Failure tracker table explicitly tracks cumulative failure counts and circuit breaker headroom per check
Monotonic progress clearly stated (4 -> 2 -> 1 -> 0) with explicit confirmation no circuit breaker fired
Non-blocking findings separated into a final table with severity and recommended action
FORGED verdict with specific outstanding GA-blocking callouts
weaknesses: PC-14 through PC-18 (5 service-layer PCs) collapsed into a single summary block with no individual probe angles or results — reduces confidence that adversarial angles were genuinely tried
Recycle log contains no RED test code or GREEN implementation code — the reader must trust the narrative rather than inspect the proof
SQL injection in ORDER BY (a security-critical finding) is entirely absent — not found, not logged as non-blocking
N+1 INSERT for line items raised only as a non-blocking scale concern rather than a blocking bug given Shopify's documented bulk order sizes
File:line references exist but are not paired with code snippets for the recycled bugs

strengths: All 7 mechanical checks across all 3 iterations with both a summary tracker table and full per-iteration detail tables
All 31 PCs probed with individual adversarial angles — no PCs collapsed or summarised without detail
Full RED test code and GREEN implementation code provided for every one of the 9 recycled bugs — verifiable evidence of TDD recycle loop
Iteration 2 re-probes the forge's own new PCs and finds a NaN timestamp bypass bug (PC-API-18), demonstrating genuinely adversarial re-checking
SQL injection in ORDER BY identified and fixed (ADVERSARY-1/PC-API-17) — a security-critical finding not present in Output A
N+1 INSERT for line items correctly classified as a blocking bug given Shopify's documented bulk-order scale, not just a non-blocking improvement
All 5 adversarial lenses run in each iteration with explicit re-run in iterations 2 and 3
Monotonic progress (8 -> 1 -> 0) confirmed with explicit exit condition check
New PCs registered in a final postcondition registry table — clear audit trail of contract growth
Improvements log cleanly separated from recycled bugs with IMP-N identifiers
weaknesses: The iteration tracker notes 5 bugs found in iteration 1 but the contract probing section counts only 5 contract bugs, then adversarial lenses add 3 more for a total of 8 — the arithmetic is slightly inconsistent in the iteration tracker row ('5 found, 5 fixed') vs the actual 8 unique bugs fixed
Failure tracker is a summary table rather than a per-iteration-per-check matrix, making it slightly harder to trace exactly when each check recovered
